learn.title: Carbon Markets Drive Clean Energy
learn.intro: Carbon markets create financial incentives for reducing emissions by trading carbon credits. This system encourages investment in clean energy, helping countries meet climate goals while fostering innovation in sustainable technologies.
learn.c1_text: Companies earn credits for reducing emissions. These can be sold to others, funding projects like solar farms. India's carbon market pilot aims to mobilize $1 billion annually by 2030.
learn.c2_text: Carbon pricing makes fossil fuels costlier, pushing adoption of renewables. The EU Emissions Trading System reduced power sector emissions by 35% since 2005.
learn.c3_text: India's carbon trading framework, part of its 2030 climate targets, could attract $20 billion in clean energy investments. States like Gujarat are piloting markets for solar and wind projects.
learn.stat1: India's coal power plants emitted 1.1 Gt CO2 in 2023
learn.stat3: India's carbon credit trading pilot to launch in 2026
learn.stat4: 1 Gt CO2 = 1 billion metric tons of emissions

learn.title: Virtual Power Plants Manage Energy
learn.intro: Virtual power plants (VPPs) use AI to coordinate distributed energy sources like rooftop solar and batteries. This balances grid demand in real-time, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
learn.c1_text: VPPs aggregate 10,000+ solar panels and 2,000 batteries in a single network. AI predicts energy needs, optimizing usage during peak hours.
learn.c2_text: VPPs respond to grid signals in milliseconds, replacing traditional generators. Australia's largest VPP manages 25,000 homes, cutting grid costs by 20%.
learn.c3_text: India's VPP market could grow to 10 GW by 2030. Tamil Nadu's pilot project uses 500 solar rooftops to stabilize the grid, saving $15 million annually.
learn.stat3: Tamil Nadu's VPP saves 12 million kWh/year

learn.title: Demand Response Balances the Grid
learn.intro: Large consumers like factories reduce energy use during peak demand, easing grid stress. This avoids blackouts and reduces reliance on backup power plants.
learn.c1_text: Demand response programs cut energy use by 15-20% during peak hours. A steel plant in Gujarat saved $2 million annually by shifting operations.
learn.c2_text: Smart systems automatically adjust energy use based on grid signals. In the U.S., demand response avoids 15 GW of peak demand, equivalent to 15 coal plants.
learn.c3_text: India's demand response programs cover 1.2 GW of industrial load. The 2022 policy mandates 10% participation from large consumers, targeting 20 GW by 2030.
learn.stat2: Demand response saves $1.5 billion/year in the U.S.
learn.stat3: India's demand response policy launched in 2022
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy

learn.title: The Duck Curve Problem
learn.intro: High solar penetration creates a 'duck curve' where midday demand drops sharply, but evening peaks require rapid ramping of fossil fuel plants. This strains grid stability.
learn.c1_text: In California, solar meets 80% of midday demand, causing a 30% drop in fossil fuel use. However, evening peaks require rapid gas plant ramping.
learn.c2_text: Less coal/fossil fuel generation reduces grid inertia, increasing frequency fluctuations. California's duck curve requires 1.5 GW of fast-response batteries by 2025.
learn.c3_text: India's 70 GW solar capacity risks duck curves. Rajasthan uses 2 GW of pumped hydro to balance midday surplus, while Gujarat plans 5 GW battery storage by 2027.
learn.stat2: California's duck curve reduces fossil fuel use by 40% at noon
learn.stat4: Evening peak demand in India: 220 GW (2024)
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy

learn.title: Perovskite Solar Cells Break Efficiency
learn.intro: Perovskite solar cells use a low-cost mineral to achieve efficiencies over 33%, surpassing traditional silicon panels. They promise cheaper, more efficient solar power.
learn.c1_text: Perovskite-silicon tandem cells combine two layers, achieving 33.7% efficiency vs. 22% for silicon alone. This could double solar output per square meter.
learn.c2_text: Perovskite cells use solution processing, cutting production costs to $0.10/W. A 2023 study showed 15% efficiency improvements over silicon in lab conditions.
learn.c3_text: India aims to commercialize perovskite cells by 2027. The CSIR-Indian Institute of Science developed a 20% efficient perovskite module, targeting $0.05/W production.

learn.title: Flow Batteries Store Grid Power
learn.intro: Flow batteries store energy in liquid electrolytes, offering long-duration storage for renewables. Vanadium and iron systems provide scalable, reliable grid storage.
learn.c1_text: Vanadium flow batteries store energy in liquid tanks. A 10 MWh system uses 500 kg of vanadium, providing 10 hours of power for 5,000 homes.
learn.c2_text: Iron flow batteries use cheaper materials. A 2023 pilot in Germany achieved 90% efficiency, with costs below $150/kWh, rivaling lithium-ion.
learn.c3_text: India's first 10 MWh vanadium flow battery in Gujarat supports 100 MW solar. The 2023 policy targets 10 GW of flow batteries by 2030, with Tamil Nadu piloting iron systems.
learn.stat3: India's flow battery target: 10 GW by 2030
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy
